name: Mermaid Live Editor
notes: Reference renderer. Always runs the latest published Mermaid. All looks, themeVariables, and CSS injection are fully supported. Use this to validate before committing to other renderers.

name: GitHub
notes: Renders Mermaid in issues, PRs, markdown files, and wikis. The %%{init}%% directive is respected and themeVariables apply. Mermaid version is pinned to a stable release. Custom web fonts are blocked by CSP. Hand-Drawn look requires Rough.js which GitHub does not bundle.

name: GitLab
notes: Similar to GitHub. Mermaid is rendered in markdown files, wikis, and descriptions. themeVariables from %%{init}%% are respected. Self-hosted GitLab instances may use older Mermaid versions. Custom web fonts are blocked by CSP. Hand-Drawn look not available.

name: Notion
notes: Notion embeds Mermaid diagrams in pages. The %%{init}%% directive is parsed but only a subset of themeVariables are applied. Mermaid version is pinned to an older stable release (10.x). Neo and Hand-Drawn looks are not available. Some diagram families may not render.

name: Obsidian
notes: Obsidian supports Mermaid via a built-in renderer and community plugins. themeVariables are respected. CSS injection may work via Obsidian CSS snippets. System fonts work; web fonts require custom snippet. Neo and Hand-Drawn look availability depends on the bundled Mermaid version.

name: Confluence + Plugin
notes: Mermaid in Confluence requires a third-party macro plugin. Plugin quality and Mermaid version vary widely. Most plugins support basic themeVariables; CSS injection is generally not available. Neo and Hand-Drawn looks are not supported.

name: Mermaid CLI (mmdc)
notes: Command-line renderer using Puppeteer + Mermaid. Produces SVG/PNG/PDF. Full feature support including all looks. CSS injection is possible via the --cssFile flag. Best for CI/CD pipelines and batch rendering.
